originalBehavior: The published route opened directly inside the Condorcet and random-forest story shell without a separate editorial hero, chapter rail, or play-first framing layer.
localStatus: The local route now adds the shared Engineering Sandbox shell with a local font stack, stronger editorial hero, chapter jump navigation, and play-first prompts while leaving the original scrollytelling DOM and compiled chart logic intact.
notes: The shell is additive and scoped through body data attributes rather than a rewritten layout system.
Chapter links are generated from data-story-chapter markers so the Condorcet, bagging, and variance sections keep their original structure.
evidence: The pilot plan requires a shared Engineering Sandbox layer with local Be Vietnam Pro and IBM Plex Mono assets, a hero, play-first prompt, and chapter jump rail.
Shared smoke coverage now checks that the route exposes the Engineering Sandbox shell before exercising the original slider and voting scenes.

originalBehavior: The article opens with a sticky scrollytelling scene where the number-of-trees and tree-accuracy sliders drive a Condorcet-style ensemble visualization.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published slider scene, sticky figure layout, and label updates while only patching shell metadata and footer policy.
notes: The original slider IDs and text labels were preserved so the compiled handlers still bind without rewrites.
The local shell no longer includes analytics or branding wrappers from the original public page.
evidence: The current plan treats bagging and feature-randomness scenes plus chart-text synchronization as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage changes both sliders and checks that the visible labels update.

originalBehavior: The core random-forest scrollytelling sequence steps through bagging, feature selection, and the gradual construction of multiple trees.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published scrollytelling section markers and the compiled tree-building runtime without refactoring the scene controller.
notes: The route still uses the original data sample, feature cards, and section-driven state transitions.
Public cleanup was limited to the intro, cross-links, and closing text.
evidence: Shared smoke coverage scrolls to the late random-forest section and verifies the click-to-predict prompt becomes active.
The parity target explicitly calls out bagging and feature-randomness scenes.

originalBehavior: Readers can click sign examples and watch each tree vote before the article reveals the majority prediction.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original test-data bag, tree traversal, prediction texts, and majority-vote display, including the interactive replay state for later examples.
notes: The click targets and vote text IDs were preserved exactly.
The local route continues to rely on the original compiled animation code rather than a rewritten controller.
evidence: Shared smoke coverage clicks a shipped test sign after the late section activates and verifies the majority label updates.
The current plan treats click-to-predict interaction and aggregation behavior as parity-critical.

originalBehavior: Later sections render barcode-style and grid-style ensemble panels that explain tree variance, feature importance, and prediction disagreement.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published barcode and cantor-style panels, with the local shell only rerouting the decision-tree links and cleaning public provenance copy.
notes: The container IDs for barcode and cantor views were preserved so the compiled bundle still mounts into the original targets.
The educational reference list remains available in the public route because it is part of the article content.
evidence: Shared smoke coverage waits for the barcode and cantor containers to populate under the shipped route.
The parity target explicitly calls out barcode-style explanation panels and aggregation visuals.

originalBehavior: The published page links back to the family decision-tree article and ends with source-oriented closing copy.
localStatus: The local route reroutes decision-tree references to ../decision-tree/, removes creator or repo callouts from the public body, and uses the shared footer as the single original-page reference surface.
notes: The route keeps local canonical metadata and no longer embeds upstream-only promo links in the body.
Technical provenance remains available in docs only.
evidence: The public-surface audit now includes MLU family checks and passes with this route included.
Shared smoke coverage confirms the shipped decision-tree link resolves to the local route.
